text.title: design-doc-examples Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-doc-examples as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: File change table without test file entries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents stop after source files and treat tests as optional. Tests must be explicit CREATE entries or they will not be implemented.

failure-mode.description: Agent copies bcrypt cost 12 or JWT 7-day expiry from example into every project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Examples encode defaults, not decisions. Always filter example technology choices through the project constitution.

failure-mode.description: Alternatives Considered table always has exactly 2-3 rows because example has 2-3 rows.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The example count is illustrative. Complex decisions may need 4+ alternatives. Prompt explicitly to consider all real options.

failure-mode.description: Agent reproduces directory tree diagrams as ASCII art from file structure examples.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Project rules prohibit ASCII art. Use directory tree format (allowed) or a file change table only.

failure-mode.description: Component hierarchy examples assume React; agent will confuse these with backend component diagrams.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Examples demonstrate correct structure but cannot show incorrect structure being caught and fixed. Filter examples by technology stack.

failure-mode.description: Examples are static snapshots; they don't reflect how a real design evolves through review cycles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Real designs change through feedback. Use examples as calibration points, not final answers.
